id: bigtech:openai codex system prompt includes explicit directive to ne:2026-04-29T19:00:58.000Z
title: OpenAI Codex system prompt includes explicit directive to "never talk about goblins"
author: Kyle Orland
summary_long: The system prompt for OpenAI's Codex CLI contains a perplexing and repeated warning for the most recent GPT model to "never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant to the user's query." The explicit operational warning was made public last week as part of the latest open source code for Codex CLI that OpenAI posted on GitHub. The prohibition is repeated twice in a 3,500-plus word set of "base instructions" for the recently released GPT-5.5, alongside more anodyne reminders not to "use emojis or em dashes unless explicitly instructed" and to "never use destructive commands like 'git reset --hard' or 'git checkout --' unless the user has clearly asked for that operation." Separate system prompt instructions for earlier models contained in the same JSON file do not contain the specific prohibition against mentioning goblins and other creatures, suggesting OpenAI is fighting a new problem that has popped…

id: bigtech:motorola reveals 2026 razr lineup with modest upgrades and h:2026-04-29T16:55:58.000Z
title: Motorola reveals 2026 Razr lineup with modest upgrades and higher prices
author: Ryan Whitwam
summary_long: Motorola is crazy about foldables. With each passing year, the company has beefed up its folding phone lineup, and in 2026, there will be four devices launching on May 21. At the top end is the company's first tablet-style foldable, the Razr Fold. Below that, Motorola will again offer three flip-style foldables: the Razr Ultra, Razr+, and Razr. Source: arstechnica.com. id: space:spacex launches 24 starlink satellites on falcon 9 rocket fr:2026-04-29T23:28:46.000Z
title: SpaceX launches 24 Starlink satellites on Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg SFB
author: Will Robinson-Smith
summary_long: Update April 29, 11:17 p.m. EDT (0317 UTC): SpaceX landed its booster. Following a successful Falcon Heavy launch from Florida on Wednesday morning, SpaceX rounded out the day by launching a Falcon 9 rocket from California. The Starlink 17-36 mission will add another 24 broadband internet satellites to the company’s massive low Earth orbit constellation. Source: spaceflightnow.com. Date: 2026-04-29. id: ai_data:asynchronous federated unlearning with invariance calibratio:2026-04-29T15:41:11.000Z
title: Asynchronous Federated Unlearning with Invariance Calibration for Medical Imaging
author: Zhaoyuan Cai
summary_long: Federated Unlearning (FU) is an emerging paradigm in Federated Learning (FL) that enables participating clients to fully remove their contributions from a trained global model, driven by data protection regulations that mandate the right to be forgotten. However, existing FU methods mostly rely on synchronous coordination. This requirement forces the entire federation to halt and wait for stragglers to complete erasure, creating significant delays due to device heterogeneity. Furthermore, these methods often face the problem that the influence of erased data is merely suppressed temporarily and resurfaces during subsequent training, rather than being genuinely removed. Source: arxiv.org. Date: 2026-04-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: nextfrontiers:what are the reasons for traffic jams whether traffic flows :2026-04-29T16:40:05.000Z
title: What are the reasons for traffic jams? Whether traffic flows or not depends on more than just the roads
summary_long: If a city's suburban railway network is expanded, additional flats are likely to be built in an agglomeration that is better connected as a result. The opposite also holds true: If new buildings spring up like mushrooms in a suburb, this will call for an expansion of the transport infrastructure. Urban development and transport therefore have a mutual relationship. Source: techxplore.com. Date: 2026-04-29. Why it matters: outline likely impact on technology, business, or society. What’s next: note near-term developments or signals to watch.

id: policy:edri responds to european commissions consultation call on t:2026-04-29T08:12:31.000Z
title: EDRi responds to European Commission’s consultation call on the Digital Omnibus
author: EDRi
summary_long: The European Commission opened consultations for the Digital Omnibus, EDRi responded by urging the Commission not to prioritise corporate interests and deregulation over privacy, transparency, and fundamental rights, ultimately putting people at greater risk of data misuse and discrimination. The post EDRi responds to European Commission’s consultation call on the Digital Omnibus appeared first on European Digital Rights (EDRi). Source: edri.org. Date: 2026-04-29.
